rulings.Case Title: TEMESGHEN TESFAY GEBREMICHAEL VS. NARENDRA SINGH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-22-602233 WITH CASE CGC-21-593369
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2025, line 1, PLAINTIFF TEMESGHEN GEBREMICHAEL'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-22-602233 WITH CASE CGC-21-593369 The unopposed motion to consolidate is granted. Temesghen Tesfay Gebremichael v. Narendra Singh et al., CGC-21-593369, is consolidated with K.C. Patel et al. v. Narendra Singh et al., CGC-22-602233 for all purposes. CGC-21-593369 shall be the lead case. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: LTC (BVI) LIMITED, A BRITISH VIRGIN ISLANDS VS. BRAUNHAGEY & BORDEN LLP, A LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2025, line 13, 1 - DEFENDANT BRAUNHAGEY & BORDEN LLP'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT Defendant BraunHagey & Borden LLP demurs to plaintiff LTC (BVI) Limited's Second Amended Complaint (SAC), causes of action one (negligence) and two (negligent misrepresentation). The court previously sustained BraunHagey's demurrer to LTC's negligence cause of action with leave to amend and overruled the demurrer to LTC's negligent misrepresentation cause of action. For the reasons stated herein, the court overrules the demurrer to the negligence cause of action and sustains it without leave to amend as to the negligent misrepresentation cause of action. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: LTC (BVI) LIMITED, A BRITISH VIRGIN ISLANDS VS. BRAUNHAGEY & BORDEN LLP, A LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2025, line 13, 4 - DEFENDANT BRAUNHAGEY & BORDEN LLP'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT Defendant BraunHagey & Borden LLP moves to strike portions of plaintiff LTC (BVI) Limited's Second Amended Complaint (SAC). The court denies the motion. BraunHagey largely targets allegations in the negligent misrepresentation cause of action. The motion is moot as to these because the court's companion order sustains the demurrer to this cause of action without leave to amend. With respect to allegations in the negligence cause of action, the court concludes that LTC's new allegations were within the scope of the leave to amend that was granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: LTC (BVI) LIMITED, A BRITISH VIRGIN ISLANDS VS. BRAUNHAGEY & BORDEN LLP, A LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Materials Filed With Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint And Motion For Undertaking
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2025, line 13, 2 - DEFENDANT BRAUNHAGEY & BORDEN LLP'S Motion To Seal Materials Filed With Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint And Motion For Undertaking BraunHagey lodged a number of documents under seal but moves to permanently seal only the SPA and descriptions of the SPA. It supports the motion with a declaration showing that the proposed sealed material is commercially sensitive. The court grants the motion in part and permanently seals the SPA (Ex. 1 to Harris Declaration ISO undertaking motion, Ex. 1 to RJN). It also permanent seals the the version of the demurrer lodged under seal on May 9. As to these documents, the court finds pursuant to Rule of Court 2.550(d) that (1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. The court denies the motion as to BraunHagey's undertaking motion. The version of that motion that is publicly filed contains no redactions that the court located. The court wonders whether BraunHagey intended to seak sealing of portions of the memorandum in support of its motion to strike, which does have redacted material, rather than the undertaking motion. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: PRECILA BALABBO ET AL VS. LANOCORP USA INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2025, line 4, PLAINTIFFS PRECILA BALABBO, EMA BELL'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment The unopposed motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment is granted. The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: SARAH SANDOVAL VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA, A CALIFORNIA PUBLIC ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2025, line 11, 1 - DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Off calendar. Amended complaint filed on May 29, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)
